_provenance._note: Themes from v4-to-v5 sweep; no new sweep conducted for v10. Findings remain valid and were incorporated into v10 grading.

themes.title: ACTIVSg10k Has No Binding Constraints
themes.summary: The MEDIUM grade network (ACTIVSg 10k) produces zero binding branch constraints in DCOPF across all tools. Maximum branch loading is 84-85%. LMPs are perfectly uniform.
key_findings: Probe-007 verified pandapower LMPs are uniform to machine precision (std = 6e-12)
Artificial congestion immediately produces meaningful LMP spreads (16.3-24.4 $/MWh)
Tests A-3, A-9, A-11, B-8, C-3, and C-10 lose discriminative value at MEDIUM scale

themes.title: case39 Uniform Costs and No Congestion
themes.summary: IEEE 39-bus has identical generator cost coefficients (c1 = 0.3 $/MWh) and no binding branch constraints, producing uniform LMPs across all 39 buses for every tool.
key_findings: A-3 TINY: All tools report uniform LMPs -- verifies OPF convergence but not LMP quality
A-9 TINY: SCOPF objective equals unconstrained OPF objective (zero cost premium signal)
SMALL network (ACTIVSg 2000) has heterogeneous costs and produces meaningful LMP spreads

themes.title: SCUC Produces No Generator Cycling on case39
themes.summary: Every tool capable of SCUC reports all 10 generators committed for all 24 hours with zero startups. Capacity-to-load ratio combined with startup costs means decommitment is never optimal.
key_findings: 7,367 MW capacity vs 6,254 MW peak load makes decommitment never optimal
A-5 is a formulation existence test rather than a UC correctness test
MATPOWER reports uniform ramp utilization (26.7%) confirming ramp constraints never near binding

themes.title: Estimated Timings Without Execution
themes.summary: PowerSimulations has four scalability tests with null wall_clock_seconds and estimated language. PowerModels scored two tests as fail without execution based on projected infeasibility.
key_findings: Probe-020 confirmed PowerSimulations C-3 was never executed (first attempt exceeded 600s timeout)
PowerModels projections were directionally correct but based on rough extrapolation (probe-016)
Protocol states: 'Record everything. For each test, record: Wall-clock time'

themes.title: Cascaded Failures Inflate Fail Counts
themes.summary: Multiple tools have scalability tests that fail solely because their prerequisite expressiveness tests failed, adding no new information but inflating apparent gap count.
key_findings: pandapower: 3 cascaded fails (C-4, C-8, C-10) vs 11 independent fails
gridcal: 2 cascaded fails (C-4, C-6) vs 14 independent fails
Proposed remedy: Add blocked_by field to result frontmatter

themes.title: PTDF Phase-Shifter Correction Gap
themes.summary: ACTIVSg10k has 5 phase-shifting transformers creating Pbusinj/Pfinj correction terms. Standard PTDF formula omits these, causing errors up to 743 MW. Correction eliminates ALL error to machine precision.
key_findings: Full equation: flow = PTDF @ (Pinj - Pbusinj) + Pfinj
Original B-9 attributions were incorrect (shunts have zero MW; island count is 1)
PowerModels handles correction internally (error < 1e-11)

themes.title: ACTIVSg10k Zero-Impedance Branch Inconsistency
themes.summary: ACTIVSg10k has 2,462 branches with zero thermal rating and 3 transformers with zero reactance. Different tools handle these differently, creating contradictory results.
key_findings: PyPSA treats zero-capacity as constraint (infeasible); others treat as unconstrained
PyPSA fails expressiveness A-3 MEDIUM but passes scalability C-3 MEDIUM after data fixes
Proposed remedy: Standardize preprocessing as protocol step

themes.title: Stochastic Perturbation Methodology Causes Excessive Infeasibility
themes.summary: The B-4/C-6 stochastic scenario methodology produces very high infeasibility rates when load and generator capacity perturbations are applied simultaneously.
key_findings: pandapower SMALL: 0.42-2.1% convergence rate (probe-006)
gridcal SMALL: 47% convergence rate
Uniform load scaling alone (0.7x-1.1x) converges reliably; combination breaks solver

themes.title: Lossy DCOPF MATPOWER Reference Validation Unachievable
themes.summary: The A-10 pass condition requires MATPOWER reference validation for lossy DC OPF, but MATPOWER's rundcopf does not have a loss option producing comparable LMPs.
key_findings: MATPOWER rundcopf is strictly lossless; post-hoc loss estimation is NOT part of optimization
Reference validation is structurally impossible as currently specified
Proposed remedy: Replace with internal consistency checks

themes.title: ACPF at 10k-Bus Is a Genuine Discriminator
themes.summary: A-2 MEDIUM produces three distinct outcome classes with strong discriminative value: converges with diagnostics, fails with minimal diagnostics, and reports false convergence.
key_findings: pandapower, gridcal, matpower: converge with diagnostics (DC warm start, convergence error)
PowerModels, PowerSimulations: fail with minimal diagnostics (NLsolve fails, Missing returned)
PyPSA: false convergence (probe-001: 0 NR iterations, 83% flat start)

themes.title: SCOPF and N-M Sweep Infeasibility at MEDIUM Scale
themes.summary: C-8 MEDIUM (500-contingency SCOPF) is a unanimous fail across all 6 tools. N-M sweep at x=5, m=4 on MEDIUM is infeasible at N-3+ for all tools.
key_findings: Even 5 contingencies time out for PowerModels at MEDIUM scale (probe-016)
Combinatorial explosion: C(1000,3) = 166M combinations
Discriminative signal is at N-1 and N-2 only

themes.title: Solver Capability vs Tool Capability Conflation
themes.summary: Multiple tests conflate open-source solver limitations with tool capability. The protocol mandates single-threaded open-source solvers, creating a ceiling independent of tool capability.
key_findings: A-5/C-4: HiGHS single-threaded MIP timeout on 2000-bus UC affects pypsa, PowerModels
C-7: Solver swap is parameter-only but qualified_pass given due to solver failures
Proposed remedy: Separate formulation expressiveness from solver scalability

themes.title: Maturity Metrics Based on Research Without Verification
themes.summary: All E-series metrics are research-based. Probe-025 debunked PowerSimulations' 100% code coverage claim (actual: 78%). Other maturity numbers are plausible but unverified.
key_findings: Probe-025 found actual coverage is 78%, not 100% as reported
Commit counts, contributor percentages, deployment claims are plausible but unverified
Proposed remedy: Require Codecov/Coveralls detail page check, not just badge
